sourceMovie.title: Last Holiday
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Last Holiday works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Interstate 60
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Last Holiday clicked for you, Interstate 60 (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Last Holiday?
faq.answer: Try Interstate 60, The Lobster, The Apartment—each shares DNA with Last Holiday while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Last Holiday?
faq.answer: Last Holiday (2006) is best described as Adventure, Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Last Holiday worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
